candidates.stmt:
I love my home City, and I live in Abbey the ward I represent and the second largest in the District. I value action over promises and am quick to question things that don't make sense or seem illogical without fear of mockery or embarrassment.

I love community action and work to try and find effective ways of bringing our community together whilst lobbying for improving our community infrastructure and provisions for young people in the area.

I work as a Sales consultant - sole trader, have two beautiful Daughters and a very supportive and honest wife, they keep me grounded. I am approachable, friendly and not scared to talk over the more contentious issues honestly offering my true feelings to those who ask. Equally I am open to changing my ideas on the world as new information and evidence present themselves. I own my mistakes, and have made a few, but none that I didn't learn from.
Being green means standing up for a city that values it's heritage and culture while fighting for a cleaner, fairer future for all.

Carholme is a historic ward. My connection goes far back, bit now we need to look forward.

I want to invest in our future, protect our spaces, and foster fairer communities.

I’m standing as your Liberal Party candidate because I believe Carholme deserves something different. I bring lived experience, fresh energy, and a genuine commitment to community, not party politics.

I’ll work with you to make change real. I’m not a career politician. I’m someone who listens, understands, and acts.

If you’re tired of the same old promises, try something different. Vote for someone who puts people before party.

RAF veteran and local teacher Darrell Harding is the Liberal Democrat candidate for the forthcoming council election in
Glebe Ward.

Darrell is well known locally for getting stuck in and taking action on local issues. Darrell lives locally with his family, just off Nettleham Road.

Darrell commented: “I'm standing this May because I want our area to be greener, cleaner and safer. From St Giles to Glebe Park, we have a strong sense of community and wonderful open spaces, but get a poor deal from the Council.
“I'm standing as a pro-active representative who will fight for our community.”

Dear Resident,
I have lived in Lincoln all my life and currently live in Hartsholme with my family. I proudly served my country for six years in the Royal Anglian Regiment, an experience that shaped my values of discipline, service and commitment.
I will always put family, community and country first.
As your Lincolnshire County Councillor for Hartsholme, I am active and accessible. I hold regular monthly surgeries at The Swanholme pub, as well as walk-in and street surgeries, ensuring residents can easily raise concerns and have their voices heard.
My local priorities are clear. I will push the City Council to take action on issues that matter to our community, starting with repairing the Black Bridge in Hartsholme Park. I also want to see more dog waste and litter bins installed to keep the area clean and tidy, alongside additional sheltered seating so the park can be enjoyed by everyone. There is also real opportunity to make better use of existing space by introducing more play equipment for all age groups.
At council level, I will put forward two key motions. The first is to strengthen planning rules to better protect flood plains and green spaces; areas that should be preserved and enhanced for community use, such as parks and football pitches.
The second is to change housing policy to prioritise homeless veterans for HMO accommodation.
I will also review council-owned assets to ensure they deliver real social value, Including supporting essential services such as post offices, pharmacies, community hubs and youth centres.
Finally, we need to revitalise our high street. This starts with the Cornhill Market; transforming it into a thriving, traditional market by reducing rents and supporting local businesses to grow, increasing footfall and bringing life back into the area.

My connection to Lincoln comes from supporting others as part of the community.

I'm motivated by fairness, sustainability & compassion - for people, animals & the environment.

I've lived in this electoral division for 8 years, so I understand local issues.

A Commando for 22 years rising to captain, then 13 years managing director in armed security, including bomb disposal.

I'm an expert in tackling waste, corruption and delivering value for money.

I will champion real improvement.

Local teacher, Cllr Matthew Fido , is seeking re-election to continue as your local councillor.

I have been proud to serve the Moorland community in City Hall for the last 3 years. As a teacher at a local secondary school, I have worked closely with young people, their families, and the wider community, putting them at the forefront of all I do. I have collaborated with local groups and churches to assist families during the cost-of-living crisis, organising collections and distributing food parcels to families in need. Through committee work, I have contributed towards the council’s communication about cost-of-living support to families most in need.

I am an advocate for our local environment. Lincoln is fortunate to be blessed with many green spaces and waterways running through the city. It is important that everyone does their bit to look after the environment we live in so we can all be proud of our community. I regularly report issues around fly-tipping and dog fouling to keep our community looking pretty and feeling safe. This is on top of reporting potholes and broken streetlights to the County Council. Lincoln is an ever-growing city, and thus needs infrastructure investment to help keep growth sustainable. I am proud to have been part of the Conservative administration on the County Council that has delivered the Lincoln Eastern Bypass. Conservative councillors are also working together to address issues such as anti-social behavior and the impacts of the Western Growth Corridor development.

By re-electing me, Matthew Fido, in May, you will be getting a dynamic councillor representing our community in City Hall, making sure everyone’s voice is heard, not just another Labour councillor who nods through decisions like cancelling our Christmas Market.

I have lived and worked in Lincoln for most of my life and have campaigned on environmental issues for many years. The climate crisis demands urgent international action and co-operation but, after the years of needless austerity, we need action on the cost-of-living crisis, transport, housing, the NHS, homelessness and so much more. With years of campaign experience as a local Green Party candidate and a passion for helping people and the environment, I would be honoured to represent my city in parliament

As a local resident, I would be honoured to be chosen to represent the area. I believe it's time to put people and the environment at the heart of decision making. We need to take action to address the climate emergency, ensuring that changes are fairly to everyone.

As a local councillor I would like help improve things for people in my ward, and also contribute more widely to making improvements across Lincoln.

That means looking at local spending and making sure people are getting the support they need, and that we're supporting facilities like libraries and youth centres, to give people their own safe community spaces.

I also think it's important to look longer term, and prioritise net zero plans so Lincoln can be at the forefront of new developments and technology, rather than being left behind to deal with the consequences of inaction.

Protecting green spaces, and ensuring any development is appropriate will help preserve the character of our area, and it's important that any development includes sufficient infrastructure and facilities, to meet the needs of a growing number of residents. Alongside this we have fantastic areas for wildlife, and it's important we protect these areas and look at how we can make them even better. I'd love to make Witham a hedgehog friendly zone, and get residents involved in thinking how they can adapt their gardens and other outdoor spaces to help protect these amazing creatures.

I enjoy meeting different people, listening to their views, and working together to find solutions to problems. As deputy convener of the Green Party heritage and tourism policy working group, I've been able to use my experience in the heritage sector to help develop policies, and I'm keen to support local heritage and the arts in Lincoln as a councillor.
